trajectory_summary: The call never opened. Meg entered a feature-debate loop within 30 seconds of Rocco's first objection and never escaped it. The utility DQ at 02:51 ended the call cleanly, but the call was already lost before that — Rocco was fully closed by 02:33. The DQ is the correct procedural exit, but it arrived 90 seconds too late and only after a debate that entrenched his resistance.
outcome_dispute: The v1 outcome tag is unknown. Based on the transcript, the correct tag is DQ — utility provider (Valley Electric, not in Ion's service area). However, the call also exhibits an unconfirmed homeownership status (see 01:38 cherry-pick) that may represent a separate hard DQ dimension. The utility DQ is the documented exit reason.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Yeah. So, basically, what we're doing here is we're building solar designs so people can see how solar will lower their electric bills.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Meg skipped the verify beats entirely and went straight to a product pitch after Rocco denied the form. The utility DQ that ended the call at 02:51 was available at 00:38 if she'd run the call_shape beat sequence correctly — intro, then verify, then pitch. Running verify first would have surfaced the DQ in under 60 seconds or opened a productive path anchored to Rocco's actual situation. Instead, Meg spent 90 seconds in a feature debate with no qualification data, against a customer who had no reason to trust her. This is the structural error that made every subsequent move harder.

key_moments.quote: Oh, okay. I've I've got it here for a 1320 Greta Boulevard.
key_moments.stewart_read: Rocco denies submitting a form. Meg's response is to read back the address rather than acknowledge the confusion and re-establish legitimacy. The schema's standard intro requires referencing the form submission to legitimize the call — when the customer denies the form, the legitimacy anchor is gone and needs to be rebuilt before anything else. Meg skips the rebuild entirely.

Rocco's 'I don't want any of those' at 00:51 is a cold first-30-seconds refusal — the hello_objection protocol applies. The correct move is acknowledge_then_small_ask: validate that he doesn't know Meg from anyone, then ask for 30 seconds to explain why she's calling. Instead, Meg asks 'any specific reason why?' — an open-ended probe that invites Rocco to build his case against solar, which he does at length. This is not the changed_mind rebuttal scenario (which presupposes prior interest); it's a cold gate that needed a small-ask, not an interrogation.

key_moments.quote: It our neighbor on the street has it. And, you don't pay how much for the electric, but still he's paying for the for the solar energy panels and everything like that. He's losing money.
key_moments.stewart_read: Rocco's neighbor anecdote is a concrete, lived objection — not an abstract concern. The high-leverage move here is to validate the observation ('That's a fair thing to notice') and then redirect to Rocco's specific situation rather than arguing about the neighbor's system. Meg counters directly ('No. Not at all.'), which signals to Rocco that his real-world experience is being dismissed. The objection_inversion play — 'What your neighbor is paying for is the gap between what his system covers and what the utility charges for the rest; the design we'd build for you is sized to your usage' — would have acknowledged the concern while opening curiosity. No fabricated numbers needed; the move is structural.

key_moments.quote: I mean, but I'm still paying for the panels and the solar energy if it's not my house.
key_moments.stewart_read: The phrase 'if it's not my house' introduces potential homeownership ambiguity. Meg never confirms whether Rocco owns the property at 1320 Greta Boulevard. The schema marks address_homeowner_confirmed as hard-required with hard_dq_if_missing: not_homeowner. If Rocco is a renter, the call was already a hard DQ on a separate dimension before the utility question was ever asked. Meg does not probe this — she continues the feature debate. The ambiguity is unresolved at call end.
